title: Tapping to Unlink Rest from Laziness
subtitle: A daytime tapping practice for releasing the belief that stopping means falling behind
excerpt: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 26 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script targets the pattern of tying worth to output — where stopping feels irresponsible and rest requires justification. The reframe moves toward rest as a responsible, productive act that serves everything else.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 26 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use during the day when guilt about resting is making it hard to stop, or as a preparation for a planned rest that feels unearned.

content.text: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 26 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The script targets the worth-equals-output belief: where slowing down feels like falling behind, and stopping requires a reason beyond 'I need to stop'. The reframe moves toward rest as part of the work, not a pause from it.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I still feel guilty for resting, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though slowing down feels unproductive, I honour the effort it takes to change that belief.
Even though I've tied my worth to how much I do, I am open to redefining what success looks like.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: Rest is part of responsibility, not avoidance.
Side of eye: The world doesn't collapse if I pause.
Under eye: I am more capable when I recharge.
Under nose: My body is not a machine — it needs rhythm.
Chin: I refuse to apologise for taking care of myself.
Collarbone: I am allowed to rest without explanation.
Under arm: Rest is productive in ways stress cannot be.
Top of head: I am safe to stop proving and start living.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: Rest makes me sharper and kinder.
Side of eye: Balance brings out my best.
Under eye: I honour my limits as wisdom.
Under nose: Guilt has no place in self-care.
Chin: I am proud of choosing recovery.
Collarbone: I rest because I am valuable, not to earn value.
Under arm: Stillness restores my capacity.
Top of head: I rest freely and without justification.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 26 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
